channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok Fails to Halt Deadline for Sale or Ban in US: Appeals Court Rejects Request
item.description: TikTok's appeal to halt deadline for sale or ban in US rejected by federal appeals court; company to appeal to Supreme Court
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cryptocurrency Taxation Bill Proposed in Texas Legislature
item.description: Texas lawmakers propose bill to regulate cryptocurrency taxes and provide clarity for digital asset owners.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: Feds propose protection for giant salamanders devastated by Hurricane Helene
item.description: A giant salamander that evolved on the supercontinent Pangaea and outlived the dinosaurs has been proposed for federal protection by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Thiel says he won't have 'full-time' role in second Trump administration
item.description: Tech billionaire Peter Thiel said he won’t have a "full-time" role in the second President-elect Trump administration, adding that he would become “depressed and crazy” if he spent his “whole life” thinking about politics.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New Zealand scientists suspect specimen of world's rarest whale died from head injuries
item.description: Scientists suspect the first complete specimen ever recorded of the world’s rarest whale died from head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: No support or updates for Windows 11 on machines not meeting minimum hardware requirements, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft has offered a miniscule concession to users determined to install Windows 11 on PCs that don’t meet its minimum hardware requirements: you will be able to do it, but on your own head be it should things go wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple updates MDM tools for new Apple Intelligence features
item.description: Apple has introduced significant improvements for enterprise IT admins in the newly-released iOS 18.2, including the power to manage the latest salvo of additional Apple Intelligence features and more. Here’s a swift look at what’s new.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DOJ asks court to reject TikTok bid to block ban
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) asked a federal appeals court Wednesday to reject TikTok’s bid to put a law that could ban the app on hold while it appeals to the Supreme Court. The law, which requires TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the app or face a ban on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Meta donated $1M to Trump inaugural fund after Zuckerberg met with president-elect
item.description: Meta donated $1 million to President-elect Trump's inaugural fund, the company confirmed to The Hill on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV: Pioneering the future of AI-powered public safety
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s AI coding entry with Jules signals tougher competition in coding tools
item.description: Google has made a significant entry into the competitive AI coding tools market with Jules, an experimental coding assistant aimed at enhancing developer workflows and challenging established tools like GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google launches Gemini 2.0 AI models, and showcases their powers in new agents
item.description: Google on Wednesday gave the public and developers a taste of the second generation of its Gemini frontier models, and a preview of some of the agents it will power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google claims breakthrough with Willow quantum computing chip but no real-world use yet
item.description: Google’s announcement on Monday of its new quantum chip, called Willow, was full of eye-catching statements. For starters, Willow took less than a minute to perform a benchmark computation that would take one of today’s fastest supercomputers 10 septillion years to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 8 brilliant browser tab tricks for Windows power users
item.description: Web browsers are essential for everything from business productivity to social media and online shopping. Whether you use Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Mozilla Firefox, Brave, or something even more exotic, there’s a good chance your web browser is your most used Windows program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, Fox 5
